title: Meeboldia digitata
intro: Meeboldia digitata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Meeboldia digitata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Meeboldia digitata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to utilize its health benefits, although its use is primarily limited to local and indigenous communities.
taxonomy: Meeboldia digitata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Meeboldia, which is a monotypic genus containing only one species. This genus is part of the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, which consists of a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
